title: Trump says Iran's supreme leader Khamenei is dead, without confirmation from Iran
subhed: 'Khameini, one of the most evil people in History, is dead,' Trump said on his Truth Social network, announcing that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was targeted and killed in air strikes. 'He was unable to avoid our intelligence and highly sophisticated tracking systems.'
byline: Le Monde with AP and AFP

title: Iran's state media say 100 killed in strike on girls' school, but losses among the regime's leaders still lack transparency
subhed: Footage broadcast by state media shows the rubble of a girls' school in Minab where 108 people were reportedly killed, according to Iranian press reports. Tehran has accused the United States and Israel of an 'atrocious crime.'
byline: By Ghazal Golshiri and Marie Jo Sader

title: Trump gives in to the temptation of regime change in Iran with Operation Epic Fury
subhed: The American president, in coordination with Israel, has embarked on a war with an unpredictable duration. This runs counter to his promises to the MAGA movement, which has opposed any prolonged military engagement abroad.

title: Iran attacks luxury hotels and airports in Dubai
subhed: Explosions rocked many of the city's major landmarks and revenue-earners, including the Palm Jumeirah man-made island, the Burj Al Arab ultra-luxury hotel and Dubai airport.

title: Israel rallies in national unity to support attack on Iran
subhed: Ahead of October parliamentary elections, on Saturday, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's opponents voiced their support for the US-Israel joint operation against Iran's regime. Israeli public opinion was also ready for a new offensive.

subhed: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen fast-tracked the announcement on Friday, much to France's dismay.

title: How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhed: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.
byline: By Laurent Carpentier and Aureliano Tonet

title: Ukraine: 'The war could still last for years and paradoxically, time is not on the Kremlin's side'
subhed: Researcher Dimitri Minic tells Le Monde in an interview that it is 'too soon to write off Ukraine,' even though the balance of power currently favors Russia.
